af_score_components.notes: New Relic NerdGraph (GraphQL) + REST APIs with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Multiple API surfaces (NerdGraph, REST v2, Insights API) requiring different auth keys for each — significant agent friction. Data ingest vs data query use different authentication keys. NerdGraph rate limit of 3 req/s is very restrictive for agent polling workflows. Account ID required separately from API key. NRQL is proprietary query language agents must learn. EU region requires different API endpoint. No retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise observability with HTTPS enforced; SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001, HIPAA, FedRAMP authorized. Scoped API keys with user vs license key separation. NerdGraph uses user keys; REST uses different key types; ingest uses license keys — well-separated concerns for security. API keys via env vars as standard practice. FedRAMP authorization demonstrates government-grade security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with documented SLA; FedRAMP authorized. NerdGraph GraphQL evolves but maintains backward compatibility. Conservative API changes for enterprise observability platform. NerdGraph 3 req/s rate limit severely constrains agent polling loops — designed for human queries not high-frequency automation. No idempotency support. No retry guidance. Multiple API surfaces increase integration complexity and failure modes.

af_score_components.notes: News aggregation API with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Simple, clean API with good documentation. IMPORTANT: Free tier prohibits commercial use — most production agents technically violate ToS. Articles are snippets, not full text — agents needing full content must scrape source URLs. Free tier limited to articles from last 30 days. Daily rate limits (not per-minute) mean burst usage can exhaust quota quickly. Source availability varies — not all sources indexed. Offset pagination with full idempotency.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial SaaS with HTTPS enforced; GDPR compliant. Single API key per account — no scope limitation. API key can be sent as header (X-Api-Key) or query param (prefer header to avoid URL logging). No fine-grained scope control. GDPR compliance for personal data handling in news queries. Standard env var pattern for API key storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial SaaS with GDPR-compliant operations. Stable, focused news aggregation API. Full idempotency for search queries. Offset pagination. No retry guidance documented. Daily rate limits can be exhausted by burst usage patterns. 30-day article window on free tier limits historical research. Source coverage gaps are an inherent data reliability concern.

af_score_components.notes: Official Vercel/Next.js DevTools MCP server for development-time diagnostics, automated upgrades, route inspection, and browser testing via Playwright. MCP server quality 80/100. Development tool only — not for production monitoring. Requires Next.js 16+ for full runtime diagnostics; earlier versions get limited functionality. Node.js v20.19+ required. init tool must be called FIRST before other tools to load documentation context. upgrade_nextjs_16 and enable_cache_components make file modifications — use in version-controlled repos only. Playwright browser dependency must be installed separately.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local development tool — no TLS or authentication required, which is appropriate for single-developer local use. File modification tools (upgrade_nextjs_16, enable_cache_components) make irreversible changes — version control is the safety net. Official Vercel project with Node.js 20+ requirement for security patches. No credentials required to operate. Playwright adds browser dependency and potential credential exposure if used in CI with sensitive browser sessions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Requires Next.js dev server running — inherently ephemeral. Official Vercel project with versioned releases. Next.js version dependency (16+ for full features) is a compatibility constraint. Partial idempotency. init tool order dependency is a usability reliability concern. Playwright browser test dependency adds external reliability risk. File modifications should be committed before running upgrade tools.

af_score_components.notes: Self-hosted Nginx web UI with MCP interface for AI-assisted configuration management. MCP server quality 55/100 — sparse MCP documentation, capabilities not fully enumerated in README. Default admin/admin credentials must be changed IMMEDIATELY on deployment — critical security risk. AGPL-3.0 license requires open-sourcing modifications if distributing. Web terminal access significantly expands attack surface if exposed to untrusted networks. Multi-server cluster management requires careful network security. No SSO documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: Platform manages Nginx SSL/TLS including Let's Encrypt automation. However, the UI itself has significant security concerns: default admin/admin credentials, no SSO/MFA, web terminal access (high-risk capability exposing full server shell), and no fine-grained scope control — any authenticated user = full Nginx admin. AGPL-3.0 with Go-based newer project; limited security audit history. Multi-server cluster requires careful network access control between nodes.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Newer project with active development — some instability expected. AGPL licensing implications for long-term viability. Configuration versioning with backup/comparison/rollback is a positive reliability feature. No retry guidance. Idempotency unknown. Single point of failure for Nginx management across all managed servers.

af_score_components.notes: Free OSM geocoder with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). CRITICAL: Commercial use of the public Nominatim instance violates usage policy — must self-host for commercial applications. Must include valid User-Agent header — requests without it are blocked. 1 req/second rate limit on public instance requires explicit agent throttling. No API key = no way to increase limits without self-hosting. Empty result set returns HTTP 200 with empty array — agents must check result count not status code. Self-hosted setup requires 100+ GB disk space for planet import and significant RAM.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public Nominatim instance uses HTTPS; GDPR compliant for handling address/location data. No authentication on public instance — no access control at API level. Usage policy (non-commercial, User-Agent required) provides some behavioral control. No API keys means no credential management needed but also no access restriction capability. Self-hosted deployments have no auth by default — must add via reverse proxy. GDPR considerations for geocoding personal addresses.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Public instance: community-run with no formal SLA; availability depends on OSM infrastructure. Self-hosted: operator responsibility. Mature, stable geocoding software with slow API evolution. Full idempotency for geocoding queries. No retry guidance. 1 req/s rate limit requires explicit throttling logic in agents. HTTP 200 on empty results creates error detection complexity. OSM coverage quality varies by region affecting data reliability.

af_score_components.notes: Notion workspace API with official MCP server (makenotion/notion-mcp-server, quality 80/100). Integration token for agents = simple bearer token. Pages and databases MUST be explicitly shared with the integration — agents get 404s on unshared content (security by design, but operational friction). Rate limit of 3 req/s is very low for database processing. Block children require separate API calls — fetching a full page requires N+1 requests. Deeply nested blocks require recursive fetching. No webhooks — agents must poll. Filter syntax for database queries is verbose and non-standard. Retry guidance documented. Cursor pagination.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial SaaS with HTTPS enforced; SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Integration must be explicitly connected to each page/database — excellent access control model. Integration secrets via env vars (not shown again after creation). OAuth2 for public apps with scoped permissions. Pages not shared with integration return 404 rather than leaking metadata. Explicit sharing model prevents accidental data access.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with SLA; SOC2 compliant. Versioned API with backward compatibility. No idempotency support — duplicate writes create duplicate content. 3 req/s limit constrains any agent processing large databases. Block N+1 request pattern for full page content increases latency and rate limit pressure. No webhooks forces polling. Retry guidance documented. Cursor pagination available.

af_score_components.notes: Official Notion MCP server (makenotion/notion-mcp-server) with quality 78/100. Read/write access to pages, databases, blocks, comments. Integration must be explicitly added to each page/database before access is granted — agents get 404s on unshared content. Rate limit 3 req/second is very restrictive for automated workflows. Block model is complex — rich text formatting requires understanding block types; page content is blocks, not markdown. No webhook support — must poll for changes. Search only works on pages/databases the integration has been granted access to. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial SaaS with HTTPS enforced; SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Same security model as Notion API: integration token for agent access with explicit page/database sharing required. OAuth2 for multi-workspace apps. Explicit connection required for each resource — prevents accidental data access. Integration tokens via env vars. HIPAA compliance available on Enterprise plan.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with SLA; SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. No idempotency support. 3 req/s rate limit requires careful throttling. Complex block model requires recursive N+1 fetches for full page content. No webhooks forces polling. Retry guidance documented. Search limited to connected pages. Cursor pagination available.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: npcpy is an MCP CLIENT that can consume MCP servers, NOT an MCP server itself (has_mcp_server=false). mcp_score=0. Python framework for building multi-agent systems across multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama). Documentation quality 65/100, error message quality 50/100. Multiple install variants (core, lite, local, all) — wrong variant causes missing dependency errors. Flask REST API for serving is basic and not production-hardened. Fine-tuning and genetic algorithm features have significant compute/GPU requirements. NPCArray features are experimental.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local Python library; TLS depends on target LLM API providers. API keys for multiple LLM providers stored in .env file — single file compromise exposes all provider credentials. Flask REST API not production-hardened for high-traffic or multi-user deployments. MIT license. Multiple LLM provider dependencies; experimental features may not follow security best practices. No scope control on API key usage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local library with no SLA. Community project; NPC-Worldwide org; active but newer. Multiple install variants create fragmentation and dependency confusion. Experimental fine-tuning and NPCArray features may have breaking changes. Flask REST server is basic — not suitable for production high-traffic. No retry guidance. No idempotency documentation.

af_score_components.notes: Go scaffolding CLI that generates structured Go projects with layered architecture and dependency injection via Wire. NOTE: has_mcp_server=true in data, but MCP server support is mentioned but NOT documented in the README — capabilities unknown and unverified. mcp_score=35 reflects this uncertainty. No auth required for CLI tool (appropriate for scaffolding). Generated projects include JWT + Casbin RBAC support. Error message quality 50/100. CLI docs are good (75/100). Generated project structure is opinionated — agents must respect existing layout conventions.

security_score_components.security_notes: CLI scaffolding tool — no TLS or auth required for the tool itself. Generated projects support JWT and Casbin RBAC (security-positive defaults). MIT license; Go project with Wire, Gin, GORM dependencies. Community project with limited security audit. MCP server capabilities are undocumented, making security assessment impossible for that component. Generated projects use Viper for config management (credentials external to code).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: CLI tool with no uptime SLA — local development tool. Go project with versioned releases and MIT license. Scaffolding tools can have opinionated churn as Go ecosystem evolves. Undocumented MCP server creates reliability uncertainty. Generated project structure is stable once created. Idempotency unknown for MCP server. No retry guidance.

af_score_components.notes: Obsidian plugin creating a secure MCP bridge for semantic search over vault notes and Templater template execution. MCP server quality 72/100. Requires three plugins: obsidian-mcp-tools, Smart Connections (semantic search), and Templater (template execution) — dependency chain adds setup complexity. Server binary is auto-downloaded on first use (startup delay). Read-only access — agents cannot write notes back to vault (security feature). API key via Local REST API plugin stored in platform-specific credential storage. Error message quality 55/100.

security_score_components.security_notes: All communication encrypted; API keys stored in platform-specific credential storage (OS keychain). Read-only vault access is a strong security feature preventing accidental note modification. Local tool keeps vault data on device. Requires Local REST API plugin with API key for authentication. Community project (jacksteamdev) — limited formal security review. Dependencies on Smart Connections and Templater plugins add supply chain surface area.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Requires Obsidian app to be running — inherently session-dependent. Community plugin with reasonable update cadence. Plugin dependencies (Smart Connections, Templater) introduce additional update risk and compatibility concerns. Auto-downloaded server binary may cause delays on first use. Idempotency: yes (read-only operations). Read-only access limits potential damage from errors. No retry guidance documented.
